summary: Flash attention via kernel_lib helpers. Compute: matmul_block(QK^T transpose, num_k_blocks=1) -> reduce MAX ROW -> BinaryMax running-max -> sub/scale/exp chain (Col bcast) -> reduce SUM ROW -> matmul_block PV -> two-op rescale of prior l,O (no L1-acc, no runtime ping-pong: templated step fn on parity). Reader: Q held, K read grid-transposed, V natural, scalers x2 (MAX+SUM pool-aware). Work split B*Hq*q_num_chunks via split_work_to_cores. Chunk sizes derived as largest divisor of Sqt/Skvt <= knob to avoid partial chunks.

detail: acceptance test 32/32 pass in both --dev and production mode (no race). Debug file 4/4 pass. Flash attention online-softmax via kernel_lib helpers; multi-core split over B*Hq*q_num_chunks.

summary: Eliminated A/B ping-pong: running (m,l,O) held in fixed CBs updated in place (read old via HeldBulk, pop, push new). Single kv_step instantiation (no parity template, no constexpr-K unroll) fits kernel-config buffer for long sequences. k_num_chunks moved to RT. Now passes 4096-seq (64 KV chunks) and 48-core golden shapes plus acceptance 32/32 dev+prod.
